title: Build a multi-agent system: A2A & Agent Registry
summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates the Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol, described as "HTTP for AI agents," and Agent Registry for discovering and orchestrating multiple agents across an organization. The tutorial covers agent card discovery, sync/async communication, and solves agent sprawl through centralized agent registration and dynamic discovery without hardcoded URLs.
writeup: This video walks through Google's Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol, which handles discovery and communication between AI agents at scale. It explains the Agent Registry, which enables dynamic agent discovery without hard-coded URLs, and agent cards, which carry agent metadata. The protocol supports both synchronous and asynchronous communication patterns. The relevance is structural: as multi-agent systems grow, a registry and standard metadata format let agents find and coordinate with each other rather than relying on fixed endpoints.
